summary: Phase 4.A.2 (sandbox-verifiable atlas implementation) complete. The deterministic art pipeline lands here: 10 recipe primitives in src/atlas/primitives.ts (paletteIndex, paletteSwap, paletteGradient, bayerThreshold, valueNoise2D, rectMask, circleMask, lineMask, columnShift, scanlineResidue) with hash2D mixer; 7 recipes under src/atlas/recipes/ (floor, wall, door, monster.ice.daemon, item.cred-chip, npc.ripperdoc, player); custom indexed-PNG encoder in src/atlas/png.ts using fflate@0.8.2 deflateSync at level 1 with filter type 0 universally and chunks IHDR/PLTE/tRNS/IDAT/IEND in canonical order; 16-color cyberpunk-neon-v1 palette in src/atlas/palette.ts (entry 0 transparent, entries 1-15 fully opaque); atlas-grid placement function in src/atlas/layout.ts (registry-declaration order, 16 wide × 8 high, 1px transparent padding); manifest serializer/parser in src/atlas/manifest.ts (alphabetical keys at every level, schemaVersion=1, strict-parse mirroring parseFloor); atlas loader in src/atlas/loader.ts with the addendum-N7-pinned DEV- refusal (em-dash U+2014); CLI in tools/gen-atlas.ts; assets/atlas.png (696 bytes, sha256 d1b4a8b73d3e2c1b7cd70c26fe15a08faae5d91351d9e2e9a542ce71727b8d1a) + assets/atlas.json (1159 bytes); ATLAS_DIGEST + ATLAS_ENCODER_SINGLE_COLOR_TILE_HASH golden constants pinned in src/core/self-test.ts plus four new self-tests (atlas-cross-runtime-digest, atlas-stream-isolation per addendum B8 with __consumed.size===1, atlas-manifest-parse, atlas-encoder-cross-runtime per addendum B4); RunStreams.atlas(recipeId) accessor with addendum-decision-1a validation; atlas-preview UI section in src/main.ts with build-vs-live <canvas> comparison, free-form seed input, 4 preset-seed buttons, and __ATLAS_PREVIEW_*/__ATLAS_PREVIEW_BUILD_HASH__/__ATLAS_PREVIEW_LIVE_HASH__/__ATLAS_PREVIEW_SEED__ window flags; the atomic flip from PLACEHOLDER_RULESET_VERSION to deriveRulesetVersion(rulesText, atlasBinaryHash) per addendum B1, executed by the vite-plugin-atlas-binary-hash plugin in the same uncommitted change set that lands assets/atlas.png + assets/atlas.json + the vite.config.ts/vitest.config.ts removal of static __RULESET_VERSION__; src/core/intmath.ts as a discovered relocation of idiv/imod from src/mapgen/math.ts (atlas needs idiv but src/atlas/** can't import from src/mapgen/**; src/mapgen/math.ts is now a backward-compatible re-export shim); the four preset-seed expectedHash goldens computed on the sandbox host (placeholder=d1b4a8b73d..., variant-A=de525492c8..., variant-B=e06d80723b..., variant-C=2fd44a69293...) pinned in src/atlas/preset-seeds.ts. Two additional discoveries during 4.A.2 implementation handled inline: (a) the assets/ directory was not being copied to dist/ during build — the existing vite-plugin-atlas-binary-hash plugin now also has a closeBundle hook that copies assets/atlas.png + assets/atlas.json into dist/assets/ so the production preview/deploy serves them at the URL the preview UI fetches; (b) a vitest define-substitution timing constraint surfaced — values must be resolved inside config() not configResolved() because vite-node substitutes literally only against the resolved-config define block; the plugin handles this by calling recompute() eagerly inside config(). Code-reviewer verdict APPROVE with 0 blockers (artifacts/code-review-phase-4-A-2.md). Three ARCHITECTURE.md doc-drifts the code-reviewer flagged (loader path, primitive set names, recipe-ID regex) patched directly during the project-lead review. The remaining 5 cosmetic suggestions (RecipeContext type colocated in floor.ts, EMPTY_SHA256 dual-source duplication trip-wire test, fflate-version explicit assertion, JSON.parse cast vs eslint-disable comment) carried forward as non-blocking follow-ups. All gates green: 821 tests passing across 53 test files; lint, typecheck, build all green; bundle 62.95 KB raw / 24.03 KB gzipped (under 75 KB); atlas binary 696 bytes (under 256 KB by 3 orders of magnitude). qa-playwright equivalent: cannot run Playwright in-sandbox (no browser binaries — same constraint as Phase 3.A.2 / Phase 4.A.1); local vite preview verified atlas served correctly with content-length 696 and matching sha256; full cross-runtime verification on chromium/firefox/webkit and the cross-OS PNG byte-equality matrix on ubuntu-latest/macos-latest/windows-latest deferred to Phase 4.B per the established pattern.
suggested_commit_message: phase-4.A.2: atlas pipeline (10 primitives + 7 recipes + indexed-PNG encoder + manifest + ATLAS_DIGEST + atomic placeholder retirement)

verification_sprint.purpose: Confirm Phase 4.A.1 foundation (HEAD of phase-4.A.1 commit 4270e4a) had not drifted before Phase 4.A.2 implementation began, and confirm the 4.A.2 implementation maintains the foundation's invariants.
verification_sprint.unit_tests: Pre-phase: 642 (639 passed | 3 skipped), 40 test files at HEAD of phase-4.A.1. Post-phase: 821 passed across 53 test files. Net-new: +179 tests across 13 new test files (tests/atlas/{params,palette,primitives,png-crc,png,layout,manifest,loader,registry,preset-seeds}.test.ts, tests/core/{streams-atlas,intmath}.test.ts, tests/build/no-transient-sentinel.test.ts) plus extensions to tests/build/atlas-binary-hash-plugin.test.ts (atomic-flip tests + RULES_FILES_PATHS drift guard + 4.A.2 file-presence) and tests/e2e/diagnostic.spec.ts (6 new atlas-preview tests + DEV-→non-DEV flip on the fingerprint assertion). The 3 RULES_FILES skip-when-absent skips from 4.A.1 are now ALL retired (the existsInPhase markers flipped from '4.A.2' to '4.A.1' since the three atlas paths now exist on disk).
verification_sprint.lint: eslint . — 0 errors. New src/atlas/** scope additions (determinism/no-float-arithmetic + SIM_UNORDERED member-access rules) verified against 4.A.2 files. The eslint-rules/no-float-arithmetic.cjs custom rule was extended with hex/binary/octal literal-prefix skip so hex constants containing 'e' don't false-positive (e.g., the 0xedb88320 CRC polynomial in src/atlas/png-crc.ts).
verification_sprint.build: vite build — green; dist/assets/index-v3IyrDD7.js = 62.95 KB raw / 24.03 KB gzipped (was 38.91/15.06 at HEAD of phase-4.A.1; the in-browser generateAtlas + atlas-preview UI added the bulk; well under the 75 KB Phase 2.0 budget; 68% headroom). dist/assets/atlas.png and dist/assets/atlas.json are produced via the plugin's closeBundle hook, mirroring the source assets/ files byte-for-byte.
verification_sprint.primary_workflow: Local vite preview (http://127.0.0.1:4173/icefall/) verified: GET /icefall/assets/atlas.png returns HTTP 200, Content-Length 696, sha256 d1b4a8b73d3e2c1b7cd70c26fe15a08faae5d91351d9e2e9a542ce71727b8d1a (matches assets/atlas.png byte-for-byte and matches the ATLAS_DIGEST golden + the placeholder preset's expectedHash). GET /icefall/assets/atlas.json returns the alphabetically-key-ordered manifest with the same atlasBinaryHash field. The atlas-preview UI section is rendered in src/main.ts via the existing render-on-load pattern; full cross-runtime Playwright verification deferred to 4.B.

atlas_pipeline_implementation.evidence: Code-reviewer verdict APPROVE with 0 blockers. All 16 verification points pass. Files (LOC): src/atlas/{params(54),palette(104),primitives(315),png-crc(40),png(158),layout(74),manifest(250),loader(115),generate(152),preset-seeds(43)}.ts; src/atlas/recipes/{floor(43),wall(35),door(38),monster-ice-daemon(45),item-cred-chip(43),npc-ripperdoc(47),player(43)}.ts; src/registries/atlas-recipes.ts(113); src/core/intmath.ts(66); tools/gen-atlas.ts(49). 13 new test files contribute ~182 net-new tests.

manifest_validates_and_atlas_binary_hash.evidence: assets/atlas.json schema validates: schemaVersion=1, top-level keys alphabetical (atlasBinaryHash, atlasSeed, generator, palette, schemaVersion, sprites), per-sprite keys alphabetical (atlasX, atlasY, recipeId, tilesHigh, tilesWide), sprite map keys alphabetical (item.cred-chip, monster.ice.daemon, npc.ripperdoc, player, tile.door.cyberdoor, tile.floor.cyberfloor_01, tile.wall.cyberfloor_01). atlasBinaryHash field equals sha256sum assets/atlas.png = d1b4a8b73d3e2c1b7cd70c26fe15a08faae5d91351d9e2e9a542ce71727b8d1a. Every sprite recipeId matches the documented regex with the seven categories pinned in src/registries/atlas-recipes.ts:RECIPE_ID_REGEX (tile|monster|item|player|npc|ui|boss). Tests at tests/atlas/manifest.test.ts cover round-trip, strict-parse on every violation type, and the non-canonical-input round-trip per addendum N15.

atomic_flip_from_placeholder_to_derived.evidence: vite.config.ts and vitest.config.ts no longer inject __RULESET_VERSION__ statically — the vite-plugin-atlas-binary-hash plugin owns the key (per addendum B1). The plugin's config() hook calls recompute() eagerly so __RULESET_VERSION__ is the derived 64-hex value when assets/atlas.png is present, or PLACEHOLDER_RULESET_VERSION when missing (no transient sentinel — addendum B1's pinned property). Built bundle dist/assets/index-v3IyrDD7.js contains the derived hex constant (verified via grep on the bundle's 64-char hex literals: d212f5cfe17ae03d03433a4119103a003f0ecfee6a2e6c0610a383d506e4473d is the build-time __RULESET_VERSION__). tests/build/atlas-binary-hash-plugin.test.ts:160-167 asserts payload.rulesetVersion matches /^[0-9a-f]{64}$/ AND not === PLACEHOLDER_RULESET_VERSION. tests/e2e/diagnostic.spec.ts:79 flipped from startsWith('DEV-').toBe(true) to startsWith('DEV-').toBe(false). tests/build/no-transient-sentinel.test.ts:25-37 scans dist/ for the forbidden '4.A.1-pre-atlas-' substring — none present. The atomic-flip soundness is confirmed in three independent senses (repository state, build-pipeline state, bundle state) per artifacts/code-review-phase-4-A-2.md:480-491.

atlas_loader_dev_refusal.evidence: src/atlas/loader.ts:67 contains the exact addendum-N7 message: 'atlas-loader: refusing to load build with placeholder ruleset (DEV- fingerprint) — re-build with \'npm run build\' to inject the real rulesetVersion'. Em-dash is U+2014 (verified). tests/atlas/loader.test.ts asserts the exact string. The loader is exported but not yet invoked by src/main.ts (Phase 5+ runtime use); only its 10 unit tests cover it currently — same defined-but-not-called pattern as deriveRulesetVersion in 4.A.1.

recipes_render_correctly.evidence: Each of the 7 recipes returns Uint8Array of length TILE_SIZE * TILE_SIZE = 256. Per-recipe golden tests at tests/atlas/registry.test.ts and tests/atlas/primitives.test.ts assert deterministic output. The encoder asserts pixels[i] < palette.colors.length per pixel (src/atlas/png.ts) with the exact pinned error format per addendum N5; tests at tests/atlas/png.test.ts exercise the throw path.

uint8array_only_and_noble_hashes_only.evidence: grep src/atlas/** for Buffer / node:buffer / crypto / node:crypto / crypto.subtle returns 0 matches in production code. The eslint.config.js src/atlas/** scope (added in 4.A.1, verified to cover 4.A.2 files) bans these imports. The encoder uses fflate@0.8.2 deflateSync (per addendum-2's fdeflate→fflate substitution); hashing uses @noble/hashes/sha256 only via src/core/hash.ts.

atlas_binary_size_under_budget.evidence: wc -c assets/atlas.png = 696 bytes. Budget is 256 KB; actual is 0.27% of budget. Estimate per memo decision 3 was 4-8 KB; actual is even smaller because the seven recipes encode well at filter 0 + level 1 deflate. JS bundle gzipped 24.03 KB; budget is 75 KB; 68% headroom.

atlas_digest_self_tests.evidence: src/core/self-test.ts adds the four self-tests with the pinned names (atlas-cross-runtime-digest, atlas-stream-isolation, atlas-manifest-parse, atlas-encoder-cross-runtime). atlas-stream-isolation uses __consumed.size === 1 per addendum B8 (NOT a 'no sim:* keys' check). ATLAS_DIGEST = d1b4a8b73d3e2c1b7cd70c26fe15a08faae5d91351d9e2e9a542ce71727b8d1a. ATLAS_ENCODER_SINGLE_COLOR_TILE_HASH = 4ea07c563df8743c7b8b4cdd9f11a4a24fa5ee60a0dbc6829f8d81a7d34b8a21.

wrap_with_skip_layout_edge_case.evidence: tests/atlas/layout.test.ts has fixtures for the row-wrap-with-skip case per addendum N9 (a multi-tile sprite that doesn't fit on the current row but fits after wrapping) plus fixtures asserting that removing the last entry preserves earlier coordinates (addendum decision 3a's coordinate-stability invariant).

non_canonical_manifest_round_trip.evidence: tests/atlas/manifest.test.ts has a test per addendum N15 that parses a non-alphabetical-key input and confirms re-serialization is byte-identical to canonical.

preset_seed_expected_hashes.evidence: src/atlas/preset-seeds.ts pins the four hex values: placeholder=d1b4a8b73d3e2c1b7cd70c26fe15a08faae5d91351d9e2e9a542ce71727b8d1a, variant-A=de525492c8e57d9a0d3a0cf0705473ea71c980fdb0531e23611b8b943e3fbb1b, variant-B=e06d80723b3a2fe417a53b908aa85a3a087e6c6ac41038bcc2fd809ddbe7dd3a, variant-C=2fd44a69293f6ad7acdff33cb8e30cc34fef4efc73ac6c79bdb3f4c0971d9233. tests/atlas/preset-seeds.test.ts asserts each one matches a fresh generateAtlas(seed) output (NOT tautological — pulls pinned hashes from the source file and compares against fresh computation). Cross-OS verification of the four hashes deferred to Phase 4.B's matrix.

discovered_work_assets_copy_to_dist.evidence: Discovered during qa-playwright-equivalent verification: the engine-builder's initial implementation produced assets/atlas.png + assets/atlas.json at the repo root, but Vite was not copying them to dist/ during build (no publicDir config + no copy hook). Result: production preview at /icefall/assets/atlas.png returned SPA-fallback HTML instead of the PNG, breaking the atlas-preview UI's build-vs-live byte-equality assertion. Resolution (minimal-diff fix landed in this same approval): extended the existing scripts/vite-plugin-atlas-binary-hash.mjs plugin with a closeBundle hook that copies assets/atlas.png + assets/atlas.json into dist/assets/. Verified post-fix: GET /icefall/assets/atlas.png returns HTTP 200 with Content-Length 696 and the expected sha256. ARCHITECTURE.md updated with the closeBundle hook description. All 821 tests still pass after the fix.

discovered_work_intmath_relocation.evidence: idiv/imod were relocated from src/mapgen/math.ts to a new src/core/intmath.ts during 4.A.2 because src/atlas/** needs idiv (for the integer-arithmetic-only paletteGradient interpolation) but the src/atlas/** layer is forbidden from importing src/mapgen/**. src/mapgen/math.ts is now a backward-compatible re-export shim — zero call-site churn for existing src/mapgen/** consumers. tests/core/intmath.test.ts (10 tests) covers the relocated functions; existing tests/mapgen/math.test.ts still passes via the shim. Mirrors Phase 4.A.1's uniformIndex relocation precedent.

doc_drift_patches.evidence: Three ARCHITECTURE.md doc-drifts the code-reviewer flagged (artifacts/code-review-phase-4-A-2.md:374-421) patched directly during the project-lead review: (1) loader path docs/ARCHITECTURE.md:612 changed from src/render/atlas-loader.ts to src/atlas/loader.ts; (2) primitive set names docs/ARCHITECTURE.md:436-447 rewritten from the earlier-draft 8-name list (paletteIndex, solidFill, pixel, outlineRect, fillRect, scanline, dither2x2, radialGradient) to the canonical 10-name list matching memo decision 1 and src/atlas/primitives.ts (paletteIndex, paletteSwap, paletteGradient, bayerThreshold, valueNoise2D, rectMask, circleMask, lineMask, columnShift, scanlineResidue plus the public hash2D mixer); (3) recipe-ID regex docs/ARCHITECTURE.md:462-475 reconciled: ARCHITECTURE.md now matches src/registries/atlas-recipes.ts:RECIPE_ID_REGEX with seven categories (tile|monster|item|player|npc|ui|boss). The memo's earlier draft regex difference is documented as historical; the implementation regex is the canonical source. Plus a fourth update: docs/ARCHITECTURE.md:591-602 amended to describe the plugin's closeBundle hook and the eager config() recompute() timing constraint surfaced during 4.A.2.

code_review.evidence: artifacts/code-review-phase-4-A-2.md: verdict APPROVE. 0 blockers. 8 non-blocking suggestions (3 ARCHITECTURE.md doc-drifts patched in this same approval; 5 cosmetic suggestions carried forward). All 16 verification points pass. Coverage: src/atlas/** at 100% lines; src/atlas/recipes/** at 100%; src/core/** at 100% lines; src/mapgen/** at 100% lines; src/registries/** at 100%; src/sim/** at 97.9% (pre-existing fallback paths from Phase 3, documented-deviation thresholds maintained). 821 tests passing.

qa_playwright_status.evidence: Phase 4.A.2's qa-playwright equivalent is the local vite preview verification (the in-sandbox agent cannot download Microsoft-hosted browser binaries — same constraint that has held since Phase 1.B; the cross-runtime CI Playwright job is the authoritative observation per the established Phase 1.B/2.B/3.B/4.B precedent). Verification performed: vite preview started on 127.0.0.1:4173, GET /icefall/ returns HTTP 200, GET /icefall/assets/atlas.png returns HTTP 200 with Content-Length 696 and sha256 d1b4a8b73d3e2c1b7cd70c26fe15a08faae5d91351d9e2e9a542ce71727b8d1a (matches the source byte-for-byte and matches ATLAS_DIGEST + the placeholder preset's expectedHash), GET /icefall/assets/atlas.json returns the canonical manifest. Full cross-runtime verification on chromium/firefox/webkit and the cross-OS PNG byte-equality matrix on ubuntu-latest/macos-latest/windows-latest deferred to Phase 4.B per the addendum.

follow_ups_carried_forward_to_phase_4_B: Acceptance criteria per docs/PHASES.md:270-275: live GitHub Pages URL serves the updated diagnostic page with the in-browser atlas-preview section working; cross-runtime Playwright job exercises the atlas-preview section on chromium/firefox/webkit (asserts window.__ATLAS_PREVIEW__ === 'ready', asserts window.__ATLAS_PREVIEW_BUILD_HASH__ === window.__ATLAS_PREVIEW_LIVE_HASH__ on ATLAS_SEED_DEFAULT, and asserts each of the four preset seeds regenerates to its pinned expectedHash); new cross-os-atlas-equality GitHub Actions job (matrix fail-fast: false, node-version: 20.x) on ubuntu-latest/macos-latest/windows-latest asserts pairwise SHA-256 equality of generated assets/atlas.png; the four preset-seed expectedHash values pinned in src/atlas/preset-seeds.ts during this 4.A.2 commit (placeholder/variant-A/variant-B/variant-C) are re-verified on the live deployment.

Phase 4.B GitHub Actions workflow extension: add the cross-os-atlas-equality matrix job per addendum N14 (fail-fast: false, node-version: 20.x). Existing deploy.yml workflow lints/typechecks/tests/builds + runs cross-runtime e2e on a single OS; the new matrix job re-runs npm run gen-atlas on each OS, uploads the produced assets/atlas.png as an artifact, and a final aggregator step downloads all three and asserts pairwise sha256 equality.
follow_ups_non_blocking_cosmetic: Code-review N4: src/atlas/loader.ts:118-125 uses a (JSON as unknown as { parse(...) }).parse(...) cast trick to sidestep the determinism/no-float-arithmetic JSON.parse ban. Prefer either an // eslint-disable-next-line comment with justification (the loader is the data-ingestion boundary; JSON.parse is appropriate) or scoping the lint rule to interior modules only.

Code-review N5: EMPTY_SHA256 + PLACEHOLDER_RULESET_VERSION are duplicated between scripts/vite-plugin-atlas-binary-hash.mjs:36-39 and src/build-info.ts:8,11-12. The duplication is forced (the .mjs plugin can't transit the @noble/hashes import graph at config-load time), but a byte-equality assertion test would close the audit-readability gap.

Code-review N6: RecipeContext type lives in src/atlas/recipes/floor.ts; six other recipe files plus src/atlas/generate.ts and src/registries/atlas-recipes.ts all import from ./floor. A src/atlas/recipes/types.ts (or src/atlas/recipe-context.ts) holding the type definition would be the cleaner home.

Code-review N7: Add an explicit expect(fflateModuleVersion).toBe('0.8.2') trip-wire to the atlas-encoder-cross-runtime self-test as a complementary explicit assertion (the encoder's golden hash already implicitly catches version drift).

Phase 3.A.2 cosmetic carry-forwards still held back from 4.A.2 per the established phase-3.B/4.A.1 follow-up rule: applyFloorEntry redundant param, dirOrdinalForStep export comment, ROLL_DOMAIN_ANCHOR_BYTES shared constant, tick unknown-action defensive check, e2e SIM_DIGEST duplication. Optional cleanup at any future phase boundary.
next_phase: Phase 4.B — live GitHub Pages verification + cross-OS PNG byte-equality matrix per docs/PHASES.md:270-275. Acceptance criteria: (1) the live URL https://porkchop.github.io/icefall/ serves the updated diagnostic page with the in-browser atlas-preview section working — verified via GET requests + Playwright cross-runtime exercising window.__ATLAS_PREVIEW__ === 'ready', window.__ATLAS_PREVIEW_BUILD_HASH__ === window.__ATLAS_PREVIEW_LIVE_HASH__ on ATLAS_SEED_DEFAULT, and the four preset-seed expectedHash assertions on chromium/firefox/webkit; (2) a new cross-os-atlas-equality GitHub Actions matrix job (fail-fast: false, node-version: 20.x) runs npm run gen-atlas on ubuntu-latest/macos-latest/windows-latest and an aggregator step asserts pairwise sha256 equality of the produced assets/atlas.png. Phase 4.B requires an external push of this 4.A.2 commit to master; after the GitHub Actions workflow runs both the existing deploy job and the new cross-os-atlas-equality matrix job and both report green, Phase 4.B approval depends on observing both green. After 4.B approval, Phase 4 (Procedural Atlas Generator) is complete and Phase 5 (Renderer + Input) is unblocked.
